0.title: User Count
0.query:
/* 
   Counts unique users: 
   extracts the frontend entity from $frontend, filters to ALL or matching RUM apps,
   builds a user identifier (tag or instance), then makes a timeseries of distinct users. 
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| fieldsAdd user = coalesce(dt.rum.user_tag, dt.rum.instance.id)
| makeTimeseries countDistinct(user)

1.title: Session Count
1.query:
/* 
  Unique sessions over time:
     1) Extract the app/entity from $frontend and keep ALL or matching apps.
     2) Build a timeseries counting distinct session IDs across that scope.
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| makeTimeseries countDistinct(dt.rum.session.id)

5.title: Browser name
5.query:
/* 
  Top browsers by unique sessions (10 max):
     1) Extract app/entity from $frontend and scope to ALL or matching apps.
     2) Group by browser.name and count distinct sessions.
     3) Sort descending and return the top 10.
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| summarize count=countDistinct(dt.rum.session.id), by:{browser.name=coalesce(browser.name, "Unknown")}
| sort count desc
| limit 10

6.title: Region
6.query:
/* 
  Sessions by country (distinct count):
     1) Extract the app/entity from $frontend.
     2) Count distinct session IDs per geo.country.iso_code.
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| summarize countDistinct(dt.rum.session.id), by: geo.country.iso_code

8.title: Avg pages per session
8.query:
/* 
  Average navigations per session over time:
     1) Extract the app/entity from $frontend and keep ALL or matching apps.
     2) Build a timeseries of the average navigation_count across sessions.
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| makeTimeseries avg(navigation_count)

9.title: Avg interactions per session
9.query:
/* 
  Average interactions per session over time:
     1) Extract the app/entity from $frontend and keep ALL or matching apps.
     2) Build a timeseries of the average user_interaction_count across sessions.
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| makeTimeseries avg(user_interaction_count)

10.title: Avg unique pages visited per user
10.query:
/*
  Returns the top 20 users with the most distinct page paths:
    1) Derive the frontend entity from $frontend and keep only matching sessions.
    2) Join per-session distinct page paths from user.events.
    3) Count distinct pages per user, sort descending, and take the first 20.
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| fieldsAdd user = coalesce(dt.rum.user_tag, dt.rum.instance.id)
| fieldsKeep user, dt.rum.user_tag, dt.rum.session.id
| join [ 
  fetch user.events
  | filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
  | fieldsKeep dt.rum.session.id, page.url.path
  | summarize pages=collectDistinct(page.url.path), by:{dt.rum.session.id}
], on:{ dt.rum.session.id }, fields: {pages}
| summarize pages=arraySize(arrayDistinct(arrayFlatten(collectArray(pages)))), by:{ user }
| sort pages desc
| limit 20

11.title: Number of sessions per ISP
11.query:
/* 
  Sessions by ISP (top 1000):
     1) Extract the app/entity from $frontend and keep ALL or matching apps.
     2) Keep sessions with an ISP value (client.isp not null).
     3) Count distinct sessions per ISP, sort descending, and return up to 1000.
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| filter isNotNull(client.isp)
| summarize count=countDistinct(dt.rum.session.id), by: client.isp
| sort count desc
| limit 1000

12.content:
### What is this Dashboard for?
Here you can analyse the sessions in your frontends and segment your audiences. If you don't see data, [start monitoring your frontends](/ui/apps/dynatrace.experience.vitals/add-frontend)

-----

13.content:
### Explore what the [new Real User Monitoring experience](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/shortlink/new-rum-experience#new-rum-experience-overview) offers.

* Use the [Experience Vitals app](/ui/apps/dynatrace.experience.vitals/) for frontend monitoring of web and mobile applications.
* Try the [Users & Sessions app](/ui/apps/dynatrace.users.sessions/) to understand how users interact with your web and mobile applications.
* Discover, track, and investigate frontend errors in web and mobile applications with the help of the [Error Inspector app](/ui/apps/dynatrace.error.inspector/).

### Additional resources

* Learn how to get the most value from your data thanks to [Dynatrace Query Language](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/shortlink/dql-dynatrace-query-language-hub).

14.title: Web sessions with errors
14.query:
/* 
  Unique error-affected sessions over time:
    1) Extract app/entity from $frontend and keep ALL or matching apps.
    2) Keep only sessions with any errors (ANR, generic errors, CSP, HTTP 4xx/5xx).
    3) Build a timeseries counting distinct session IDs for those errored sessions.
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| filter error.anr_count > 0 OR error.count > 0 OR error.csp_violation_count > 0 OR error.http_4xx_count > 0 OR error.http_5xx_count > 0
| makeTimeseries countDistinct(dt.rum.session.id)

17.title: Sessions per user type - Real vs Synthetic vs Robot
17.query:
/* 
  Sessions by user type (distinct users):
     1) Extract the app/entity from $frontend and keep ALL or matching apps.
     2) Count distinct users (dt.rum.instance.id) grouped by dt.rum.user_type.
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| summarize sessions_by_user_type=countDistinct(dt.rum.instance.id), by: dt.rum.user_type

18.title: Sessions per frontend type
18.query:
/* 
  Sessions by frontend type (distinct sessions):
     1) Extract the app/entity from $frontend and keep ALL or matching apps.
     2) Count distinct sessions grouped by dt.rum.application.type.
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| summarize sessions_by_frontend_type=countDistinct(dt.rum.session.id), by: dt.rum.application.type

20.title: Sessions with user interactions
20.query:
/* 
  Unique interactive sessions over time:
     1) Extract app/entity from $frontend and keep ALL or matching apps.
     2) Keep only sessions with at least one user interaction (user_interaction_count > 0).
     3) Build a timeseries counting distinct session IDs for those interactive sessions.
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| filter user_interaction_count > 0
| makeTimeseries countDistinct(dt.rum.session.id)

23.title: OS name
23.query:
/* 
  Top operating systems by unique sessions (10 max):
     1) Extract the app/entity from $frontend and keep ALL or matching apps.
     2) Group by os.name and count distinct sessions.
     3) Sort descending and return the top 10.
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| summarize count=countDistinct(dt.rum.session.id), by:{os.name=coalesce(os.name, "Unknown")}
| sort count desc
| limit 10

24.title: Device Type
24.query:
/* 
  Top device types by unique sessions (10 max):
     1) Extract the app/entity from $frontend and keep ALL or matching apps.
     2) Group by device.type and count distinct sessions.
     3) Sort descending and return the top 10.
*/
fetch user.sessions
| filter in(frontend.name, {$frontend})
| summarize count=countDistinct(dt.rum.session.id), by:{device.type=coalesce(device.type, "Unknown")}
| sort count desc
| limit 10
